entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: The crop Arachis hypogaea affected by late leaf spot disease

entities.name: LATE LEAF SPOT
entities.description: A disease of groundnut caused by Phaeoisariopsis personata

entities.name: PHAEOISARIOPSIS PERSONATA
entities.description: The fungal causal agent of late leaf spot disease in groundnut

entities.name: PSEUDOMONAS AERUGINOSA
entities.description: A bacterial species used for the biocontrol of late leaf spot

entities.name: GSE 18
entities.description: A specific chlorothalonil-tolerant isolate of Pseudomonas aeruginosa

entities.name: GSE 19
entities.description: A specific chlorothalonil-tolerant isolate of Pseudomonas aeruginosa

entities.name: CHLOROTHALONIL
entities.description: A fungicide used for the management of late leaf spot

entities.name: LESION FREQUENCY
entities.description: A measure of disease impact used to evaluate the efficacy of bacterial isolates

entities.name: DISEASE SEVERITY
entities.description: A measure of the extent of late leaf spot infection in the field

entities.name: POD YIELD
entities.description: The agricultural productivity of groundnut measured after treatment

entities.name: PROPHYLACTIC SPRAY
entities.description: The application method of bacterial isolates to control disease

entities.name: PEAT-BASED FORMULATION
entities.description: A specific formulation used for the delivery of P. aeruginosa GSE 18

entities.name: GLASSHOUSE
entities.description: The controlled environment where initial efficacy trials were conducted

entities.name: FIELD
entities.description: The natural environment where disease severity and pod yield were tested

entities.name: GROUNDNUT PHYLLOPLANE
entities.description: The surface of groundnut leaves where bacterial isolates survive

entities.name: RIFAMYCIN-RESISTANT MUTANTS
entities.description: Mutants of GSE 18 and GSE 19 used to study survival on the phylloplane

entities.name: IN VITRO CONIDIAL GERMINATION
entities.description: A laboratory test used to evaluate the inhibitory effect of bacterial isolates on the pathogen
